In the Bed Wars gamemode, players are split into teams. Each team has a bed. If a team's bed is destroyed, all players in that team can no longer respawn. The goal is to destroy all other teams' beds (via Pet button) and be the last team alive.

You can buy items (blocks, weapons, tool, etc.) and team upgrades (heal pool, haste, trap, etc.) in shops from the resources (iron, gold, diamond, emerald) you can collect from the generators on the map. These are useful for protecting the bed with various layers to make it harder to break it, increase your attack damage, increase your defense, or give you or your team various abilities.

In the beginning, there's a grace period, where no attacks are allowed, and players can't leave their own base.

Each player has an inventory with all of their items. Cycle through the slots using the Vanish button or by typing the number of the slot in the chat (ex. '5'). Use weapons through the kill button, other items via Pet. To start breaking someone's bed, Pet. Make sure to always have the correct item selected in your inventory (blocks when you protect the bed, tools when you break beds, etc.).
You can break beds with anything, but using the correct tool for each layer will make the process significantly faster.

If you die, you will respawn after a few seconds, unless your bed is broken. If your bed is broken, you will not respawn anymore.

In the Deathrace gamemode, there is a specific track you need to follow on the map, repeatedly. If you enter a room that isn't the next one on the track, you die! You must complete all laps first to win.\n\nSome power-ups are available next to the track, go near them to pick them up. It will tell you what they do and how to use them when you pick them up. You can store multiple power-ups and use them one at a time.

In the Mingle gamemode, the game assigns a certain number of players to certain rooms on the map. At the end of the time limit, exactly that number of players need to be in the rooms. If there are too many or too few players in a room, all players in that room die. If you are not in any of the assigned rooms, you die. Oh, and if no one dies in a round, the time limit permanently decreases. The last player alive wins!

The Coven is a Team of 1-4 people, after 3 meetings (amount replaced with hosts settings) a random Member of the Coven will gain the Necronomicon. With the Necronomicon, that Coven Member may kill normally and also has a stronger ability. After the Necronomicon was given to the first member, every meeting after will give it to another until all members of the Coven have it. Most roles in this faction are also Support oriented, meaning that they need Teamwork and coordination to work perfectly together.

The Necronomicon is an ability buff owned by a single member of the Coven. The Necronomicon goes to a different Coven member after every meeting, and for that round will buff their role's ability. Most Coven roles will state in their description what the Necronomicon buffs in their role.

<size=50%>Credit to mystic_haze2 on Discord for writing the first paragraph</size>

Neutrals do not belong to Crewmates or Impostors, and they win on their own terms. Each type of neutral wins in a different way. Neutrals work for themselves and have specific conditions depending on their role on how to win. Neutral Benigns win when their specific conditions are completed, each role having different conditions necessary to win. They do not trigger the game to end, meaning they must wait until the Crewmates, Impostors, Coven, or another Neutral to win the game and win along with them. Neutral Evils also have their own specific conditions to win, much like the Neutral Benigns. However, when they complete their objectives to win the game, they end the game and win without Crewmates, Coven, or Impostors- also called 'stealing the win'. Neutral Killers are neutrals who can kill, much like Impostors, but do not work with the Impostors. Most of the Neutral killers work on their own, although some can recruit other players to work with them. They win the game by killing off all the Impostors, Cremwates, Coven, and other Neutrals, until they are the only player remaining. Neutral Pariah roles are much like Neutral Benign roles, however their main objective is to make sure Crewmates do not win. If any team other than the Crewmates win, said Neutral Pariah role will win along with the winning team. They have special abilities to try and harm the crewmates.
